userStories.title: Project Scaffold
acceptanceCriteria: next.js 14 App Router project initialised with TypeScript strict mode
Tailwind CSS configured with PostCSS
Prisma installed and initialised (prisma/ directory with schema.prisma stub)
docker-compose.yml starts a PostgreSQL 15 container on port 5432
.env.example documents DATABASE_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL
package.json scripts: dev, build, start, lint, db:migrate, db:seed
Folder structure: app/, components/, lib/, prisma/, types/
Project builds without errors (next build)

userStories.title: Database Schema and Seed Data
acceptanceCriteria: Prisma schema defines Guitar model: id, brand, model, yearRange, category (enum: ELECTRIC|ACOUSTIC|BASS|OTHER), imageUrl, eloScore (default 1500), totalDuels (default 0), isActive (default true), createdAt
Prisma schema defines Duel model: id, guitar1Id, guitar2Id (FK Guitar), winnerId (nullable FK Guitar), category, sessionId, createdAt
Prisma schema defines Vote model: id, duelId (FK Duel), sessionId, reaction (nullable enum), createdAt
Unique constraint: one vote per (duelId, sessionId)
Migration runs cleanly: npx prisma migrate dev
Seed file (prisma/seed.ts) inserts 25+ guitar models: at least 10 electrics, 8 acoustics, 7 basses with realistic brand/model/yearRange/imageUrl data
ELO scores initialised to 1500 for all seed guitars
npx prisma db seed runs without errors

userStories.title: ELO Calculation Utility
acceptanceCriteria: lib/elo.ts exports a pure function calculateElo(winnerElo: number, loserElo: number, kFactor?: number): { newWinnerElo: number; newLoserElo: number }
K-factor defaults to 32
Standard ELO formula: expected = 1 / (1 + 10^((opponent - player) / 400))
New ELO = old ELO + K * (actual - expected), where actual is 1 for winner, 0 for loser
ELO values are rounded to nearest integer
Unit tests cover: equal ELOs, strong favourite wins, upset (weaker wins), custom K-factor
All tests pass

userStories.title: Random Duel API Route
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/duels/random returns { duel: { id, guitar1, guitar2 } } with HTTP 200
Both guitars belong to the same category
Guitar objects include: id, brand, model, yearRange, category, imageUrl, eloScore, totalDuels
Accepts optional query param ?exclude=id1,id2,...,id10 to skip recently seen duel pairs (server ignores exact pair if both IDs appear together)
Accepts optional query param ?category=ELECTRIC|ACOUSTIC|BASS|OTHER to filter by category
Returns HTTP 503 with { error: 'insufficient_guitars' } if fewer than 2 active guitars exist in the requested category
A new Duel row is created in the database with the two guitar IDs and sessionId (from X-Session-Id header or generated)
Duel ID is included in the response so the client can reference it when voting
Unit tests: same category guarantee, exclusion logic, 503 on empty catalogue
All tests pass

userStories.title: Vote API Route
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/votes accepts body { duelId: string, winnerId: string, sessionId: string, reaction?: string }
Returns HTTP 201 with { vote: { id }, guitar1: { id, eloScore }, guitar2: { id, eloScore } } showing updated ELO scores
Validates winnerId is one of the two guitar IDs in the duel; returns 400 if not
Updates both Guitar.eloScore and Guitar.totalDuels using calculateElo from lib/elo.ts
Sets Duel.winnerId on the referenced duel row
Creates a Vote row with duelId, sessionId, reaction
Enforces unique (duelId, sessionId) — returns HTTP 409 if already voted
All DB updates happen in a single Prisma transaction
Unit tests: happy path, duplicate vote 409, invalid winnerId 400
All tests pass

userStories.title: GuitarCard Component
acceptanceCriteria: components/GuitarCard.tsx renders guitar photo (next/image, fill layout), brand, model, yearRange
Accepts props: guitar (Guitar type), isSelected (boolean), isWinner (boolean), showElo (boolean), onClick callback
When isSelected=true: highlighted border/ring style
When isWinner=true: trophy or checkmark badge visible
When showElo=true: ELO score displayed below model name
onClick fires only when not already selected (prevents double-vote)
Mobile-first: card is at minimum 140px wide, photo aspect ratio 4:3
Component is accessible: button role, aria-label includes brand and model
Storybook or snapshot test captures all visual states
No TypeScript errors

userStories.title: Mode Random Page
acceptanceCriteria: app/random/page.tsx implements all five UI states: loading, ready, voted, reacting, error
loading state: skeleton cards or spinner while fetching duel
ready state: two GuitarCards side by side, no result visible
voted state: winner highlighted (isSelected + isWinner), both ELO scores shown, reaction picker visible, Suivant button visible
reacting state: one of the 5 reactions selected visually ('Choix impossible', 'Trop facile', 'Je connais pas les deux', 'Je possède une des deux', 'J\'ai joué les deux')
error state: error message + Réessayer button that retries the fetch
Vote triggers optimistic update (UI transitions to voted state immediately, POST /api/votes sent in background)
Session history stored in sessionStorage key 'guitar-match-duel-history' as array of duel pair IDs (max 10 entries); passed as ?exclude= param to API
Suivant button prefetches next duel (calls GET /api/duels/random and preloads images) while user is in voted/reacting state
Clicking Suivant transitions immediately to ready state using prefetched data
sessionId generated once per browser session (crypto.randomUUID), stored in sessionStorage key 'guitar-match-session-id'
Mobile-first layout: cards stack vertically on screens < 640px, side by side on ≥ 640px
No TypeScript errors, no console errors in happy path
